popularStory.description:
Sir James Hamilton of Cadzow, 5th Laird of Cadzow (before 1397 – c. 1440) was a Scottish nobleman and royal hostage.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow於1398年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福時，他的父親Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow28歲，母親Janet Douglas of Dalkeith23歲。他於1422年10月20日與Janet Livingston of Callendar在蘇格蘭斯林史爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1424年登記服兵役。他去世於大約1440年在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安享年43歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hamilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow was born in 1398, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow, was 28 and his mother, Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, was 23. He married Janet Livingston of Callendar on 20 October 1422, in Stirlingshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1424. He died about 1440, in Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow est né en 1398, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Janet Livingston of Callendar le 20 octobre 1422, à Stirlingshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1424. Il est décédé environ 1440, à Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow 1398 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow 28 und seine Mutter Janet Douglas of Dalkeith 23. Er hat am 20. Oktober 1422 in Stirlingshire, Scotland Janet Livingston of Callendar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1424 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb um 1440 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Midlothian, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow è nato il nel 1398 nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Janet Livingston of Callendar il 20 ottobre 1422, nel Stirlingshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1424. Morì circa 1440, nel Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzowが1398年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカドゾウで生まれた時，父Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzowは28歳，母Janet Douglas of Dalkeithは23歳であった。1422年10月20日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアでJanet Livingston of Callendarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1424年に兵役登録をした。彼は約1440年にスコットランドミドロスアンで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow(이)가 1398년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Douglas of Dalkeith(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1422년 10월 20일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르에서 Janet Livingston of Callendar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1424년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 대략 1440년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow nasceu em 1398, em Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Janet Livingston of Callendar em 20 de outubro de 1422, em Stirlingshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1424. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1440, em Midlothian, Scotland, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow родился в 1398 г. в местности кадзоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Livingston of Callendar 20 октября 1422 г. в местности стирлингшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. В 1424 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер около 1440 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow nació en 1398, en Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Hamilton - 4th Laird of Cadzow, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Janet Douglas of Dalkeith, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Janet Livingston of Callendar el 20 de octubre de 1422, en Stirlingshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1424. Murió aproximadamente 1440, en Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 43 años.
